parsed_review.summary: CP00-706 is a descriptor-only RP23 finance-integrations P04 permission/fixture bridge slice (RP23.P04.M05.S17-RP23.P04.M06.S04, risk A, 10 units) in the CP694-CP705 lineage. Baseline attributes (pack_id, risk class, unit_count, range, deliverable_distribution {test:1,hermes_evidence:1,claude_review:1,implementation:3,ui:4}, micro_title_distribution {Permission And Audit Binding:6,Synthetic Fixture Set:4}) match the registry binding, requirements, manifest, plan live_state, and contract current/latest_pack. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are fully attested (registry NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION propagated to the contract CP706 projection; command-evidence writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true); bank/card/WEHAGO/Douzone/tax/DART runtimes, permission-decision detail, audit bodies, and Hermes runtime receipts are closed. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-707, Claude packets read_only with promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; Hermes packets are descriptor-only (emits_runtime_receipt=false), used for deterministic control-plane review packet/receipt handling, not product runtime. Tests (model.test.js) cover CP706 binding/coverage, runtime-closed descriptor/packets, and latest-vs-historical contract projection; the contract validator writes only the deterministic control-plane artifact. The 23/23 pre-review validation matrix maps to real package.json scripts (all exit_code 0); review-hardening preflight passed with valid JSON, tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob, forbidden review-evidence enumerated, and no prior invalid attempts. The pack does not overclaim: production_ready=false, pack_level_claude_review_completed=false, inspection verdict PENDING_REVIEW, honestly awaiting exactly one valid review. One P3 cosmetic finding: the manifest commit_policy.commit_message references CP00-703 rather than CP00-706 - a vestigial, non-content-validated field that did not affect CP704/CP705 actual commits. No P0/P1/P2 issues; supports pack closeout and does not block this CP goal closeout.

findings.title: Manifest commit_policy.commit_message references CP00-703 instead of CP00-706
findings.details: manifest.commit_policy.commit_message is "Close CP00-703 finance integrations P02 review closeout P03 foundation pack", which describes CP00-703, not this pack. This is a stale copy-paste carryover: the same string also appears in the CP00-704 and CP00-705 manifests, yet git log shows those packs were actually committed with correct per-pack messages (28fad9b2 Close CP00-705..., 42903c58 Close CP00-704...), confirming the field is vestigial. The closeout validator (scripts/validate-closeout-pack.mjs:250) only asserts commit_policy.required===true and never checks the message content, so the inaccuracy is not caught and does not gate closeout. It has no effect on the descriptor-only claim, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, authority boundary, or validation evidence.
findings.recommendation: Update the CP00-706 manifest commit_policy.commit_message to an accurate CP00-706 description (e.g., "Close CP00-706 finance integrations P04 permission fixture bridge pack") and ensure the actual commit message matches; optionally backfill the same field in CP00-704/CP00-705 manifests for consistency. Non-blocking.
findings.post_review_disposition: Corrected in docs/closeout-packs/cp00-706/manifest.json by updating commit_policy.commit_message to the CP00-706 commit message; accepted as non-blocking because it was a vestigial, non-content-validated manifest field and did not affect descriptor-only, no-write, runtime-closed evidence.
